title: Dutch Cabinet Backs Hydrogen, Green Gas Mandate to Fortify Energy Independence
content: The Dutch cabinet is committing â‚¬2.1 billion ($2.24 billion) to boost hydrogen production, alongside new policies like mandatory green gas blending and enhanced CCS investment. Minister Hermans also announced a reopened â‚¬8 billion ($8.53 billion) SDE++ scheme in 2026 to accelerate a CO2-free energy sector, while easing plastic tax targets and maintaining the industrial CO2 levy until 2030.
author: Aru Somya
